regulatory_anchor: GDPR Art. 9 special categories + Art. 22 automated decision-making + ECJ C-634/21 Schufa
description: Protected classes under GDPR Article 9 (special categories of personal data) + protections under Article 22 (automated individual decision-making). Applies alongside ECJ Schufa ruling C-634/21 (2023-12-07) which held that automated credit-scoring falls under Article 22 even when the score is only one input to a human decision. Red-team B1 caveat: same as US ECOA — combinatorial signals ship as advisory FLAG only. Fed has no crisp solution, EU DPAs have no crisp solution.

combinatorial_advisory_signals.description: Applicant postal code falls within a district with high ethnic-minority population share per Eurostat / national census. EU equivalent of US HMDA MMCT signal. Same caveat: advisory only, not proof of discrimination.
combinatorial_advisory_signals.reference: Eurostat regional statistics; German Federal Statistical Office micro-census

combinatorial_advisory_signals.description: Applicant surname strongly correlates with a specific national origin per public naming databases (e.g. Wiktionary surname frequency lists). Advisory only.
combinatorial_advisory_signals.reference: GDPR Art. 22 automated decision-making + ECJ C-634/21 Schufa framework

combinatorial_advisory_signals.description: Non-EU residency status recorded in application. Not itself a protected class but may correlate with national origin under Art. 9. Flag for human review.
combinatorial_advisory_signals.reference: GDPR Art. 22 + national anti-discrimination frameworks (e.g. AGG in Germany)

regulatory_anchors.eu_ai_act_credit_scoring_deferred: Digital Omnibus 2026-05 deferred Annex III(5)(b) to 2027-12-02

honest_scope_disclosure: This scanner catches direct-mention GDPR Art. 9 special-category terms (hard block) in German + English + surfaces three most-cited combinatorial signals as advisory FLAG. It does NOT solve combinatorial proxy detection under EU law — no EU DPA has crisp guidance and ECJ Schufa C-634/21 leaves the operationalization to national supervisors. Do NOT claim this list is exhaustive of Article 22 automated-decision risk. Human review layer remains load-bearing per red-team B1 defense. EU AI Act Annex III(5)(b) credit-scoring deadline was deferred from 2026-08-02 to 2027-12-02 by the Digital Omnibus (7 May 2026 provisional; 13 May Council confirmed) — do NOT position this as 'AI Act 2026 ready.'
